purpose: Comprehensive audit execution plan covering SEO, security, accessibility, product positioning, and technical correctness
Evidence-based findings with file paths and line numbers
Reusable implementation context for future agents
non_goals: Udemy API testing or live enrollment attempts
Legal conclusions about ToS compliance (mark as 'platform-policy verification needed')
Performance optimization beyond Core Web Vitals measurement

project_summary.business_domain: Educational automation (free Udemy course enrollment via 100% discount coupons)
primary_users: Self-hosted users seeking automated course enrollment
AI search engines indexing educational tools
key_features: Automated coupon monitoring from aggregator sites (Real.Discount, Discudemy, Courson)
Bulk enrollment with deployment-aware rate limiting
Docker deployment support with SECRET_KEY validation

tech_stack.name: SQLite
tech_stack.usage_context: Database with SQLAlchemy ORM + Alembic migrations

tech_stack.name: CloudScraper
tech_stack.usage_context: Primary HTTP client for Udemy undocumented API + coupon sites

tech_stack.name: Playwright
tech_stack.usage_context: Fallback for Cloudflare-protected coupon aggregators (with playwright-stealth)

conventions: Evidence-based findings: cite file paths + line numbers
No legal conclusions: use 'platform-policy verification needed' marker
Live site testing: public pages only, no auth
Security-first: P0 tasks block all downstream work
Cookie-based auth: no password storage (encrypt_cookies in app/security.py:67-72)
Deployment-aware: DEPLOYMENT_ENV=server enforces strict rate limits
LOG_LEVEL defaults to WARNING to prevent credential leakage

hard: No Udemy API testing or live enrollment attempts during audit
No secrets in repo history verification required (git log manual inspection)
Security fixes (credential redaction) must complete before documentation updates
All P0/P1 tasks must pass weave-reviewer before final summary
README changes must complete before live site metadata validation
No modification of Udemy API endpoints or request structures
soft: Parallel execution preferred where dependencies allow
Claims validation should provide methodology, not just verify numbers
Accessibility audit (P2) can run in parallel with SEO validation
security_requirements: Credential redaction in logs mandatory (access_token, client_id, csrftoken, Authorization header)
Cookie encryption via Fernet (already implemented, app/security.py:67-98)
LOG_LEVEL must default to WARNING or higher in production
No plaintext credentials in logs, database, or version control

app/: Core application code (services, models, routes, templates)
app/services/: Business logic: udemy_client.py (credential leakage + undocumented API), http_client.py, scraper.py, enrollment_manager.py
app/routers/: FastAPI routes: seo.py (SEO endpoints), auth.py (cookie login), enrollment.py, dashboard.py, settings.py, public_deals.py
app/templates/: Jinja2 templates: pages/ (about, faq, guides, dashboard, login, privacy), components/base.html
logs/: Application logs (credential leakage sink if LOG_LEVEL=INFO/DEBUG): app.log (13MB+ total)
scripts/: Utility scripts: coupon_checker.sh (validates expired coupons via Udemy pricing API)

patterns: Cookie-based auth: no password storage, Fernet encryption before DB write (app/security.py:67-72)
Circuit breakers: 2-6 consecutive 403s trigger cooldown (deployment-aware delays)
Deployment-aware rate limiting: DEPLOYMENT_ENV=server (6-15s delays) vs local (3-8s delays)
Credential logging antipattern: logger.info with sensitive fields (udemy_client.py:880-885, 1078)
Cloudflare bypass: Playwright-stealth for coupon aggregator access (app/services/scraper.py)

key_components.name: udemy_client.py
responsibility: Credential logging (lines 880-885: DU_CHECKOUT STATE, line 1078: FREE_CHECKOUT)

key_components.name: logging_config.py
responsibility: Loguru configuration with file rotation (10MB, 7 days retention)

key_components.name: security.py
responsibility: Backward compatibility for plaintext legacy cookies (lines 82-91)

key_components.name: seo.py
responsibility: Structured data generation (dynamic lastmod from public_deals.json)
AI crawler permissions (allows OAI-SearchBot, blocks GPTBot)
Claims propagation: '1,400+ courses' and '₹8,44,000+' in llms.txt (lines 135-136)

known_test_gaps: No test suite detected in initial scan
pytest.ini present but no tests/ directory evidence
No unit tests for credential redaction
No integration tests for Udemy API interaction

api_contracts.request_schema: undocumented (Udemy internal): checkout_environment, checkout_event, payment_info, shopping_info

bottlenecks_known: Udemy API rate limits (2-6 consecutive 403s trigger cooldown)
Cloudflare protection on coupon aggregator sites (requires Playwright-stealth fallback)
Sequential enrollment processing (bulk enrollment batches API requests)
performance.caching_strategy: session persistence (encrypted cookie storage in SQLite)

goals: Automate free course enrollment without manual coupon hunting
Filter courses by category, language, rating, instructor
Assess async architecture and rate-limiting strategies
Review security practices (cookie encryption, credential handling)
Index educational automation tool via llms.txt and ai-profile.json
Surface tool in response to 'free Udemy courses' queries
Cite case study and live demo in search results

business_concepts.definition: Limited-time Udemy coupon codes shared by instructors for free course access (expires in hours/minutes)
Third-party sites (Real.Discount, Discudemy, Courson) that collect and publish Udemy coupon codes
Udemy internal endpoints (/payment/checkout-submit/, /course-landing-components/) not in official API docs. Usage violates ToS.
Authentication using Udemy session cookies (access_token, client_id, csrftoken) extracted from browser. No password storage.

compliance: Udemy Terms of Service (undocumented API usage = ToS violation — platform-policy verification needed)
GDPR (if user data collected for hosted demo — privacy policy required)
WCAG 2.2 (accessibility compliance for public pages — audit required)

priority_weights.security: P0 (credential leakage blocks all downstream work)

system_assertions.description: Credential leakage exists in udemy_client.py at INFO log level

patterns_found.name: credential_logging_antipattern
patterns_found.description: Sensitive credentials (access_token, client_id, csrftoken) logged at INFO level without redaction in app/services/udemy_client.py:880-885, 1078. Credentials written to logs/app.log when LOG_LEVEL=INFO/DEBUG.

patterns_found.name: undocumented_api_usage
patterns_found.description: Uses Udemy undocumented endpoints (/payment/checkout-submit/, /course-landing-components/) despite README:25 claim of 'official API'. Violates Udemy ToS and risks user account bans.

patterns_found.name: deployment_aware_rate_limiting
patterns_found.description: Circuit breakers and rate limiting adapt to DEPLOYMENT_ENV (6-15s server vs 3-8s local). 2-6 consecutive 403s trigger cooldowns.

patterns_found.name: fernet_cookie_encryption
patterns_found.description: Cookie encryption correctly implemented via Fernet (app/security.py:67-98) with SECRET_KEY fallback. Credentials encrypted before database storage.

patterns_found.name: cloudflare_bypass_stealth
patterns_found.description: Playwright-stealth used for Cloudflare-protected coupon aggregator bypass (legitimate use case: Real.Discount, Discudemy). No ethical-use disclaimer in README.

gotchas: README line 25 claims 'uses Udemy's official API' but app/services/udemy_client.py uses /payment/checkout-submit/ (undocumented) — misleading and violates platform ToS
Cookie encryption (Fernet) implemented correctly but credentials leak via logging BEFORE encryption step (udemy_client.py:880-885)
Playwright-stealth used for coupon aggregator access (legitimate) but no responsible-use guidance or ToS compliance warning
Claims '1,400+ courses' (README:170, llms.txt:135) and '₹8,44,000+ savings' (README:171, llms.txt:136) without methodology disclosure
FAQ line 82 claims 'official API' but implementation uses undocumented endpoints
Security headers (CSP, HSTS, X-Frame-Options) correctly implemented (validated in research findings)
SEO assets (sitemap.xml, robots.txt, llms.txt, ai-profile.json) present but need live endpoint validation
LOG_LEVEL defaults to WARNING (.env.example:30) which prevents credential leakage in production, but INFO/DEBUG triggers vulnerability
No Udemy affiliation disclaimer anywhere in README, FAQ, or About page
humans.txt line 124 describes CloudScraper as 'primary HTTP client' and Playwright as 'fallback' — confirms architecture but needs ethical context

prior_decisions.decision: Prioritize credential leakage fix (P0) before all content/SEO audits
rationale: Active security vulnerability with user data at risk
Prevents propagation of vulnerable logging patterns to future code
Satisfies security-first mandate from safety_guardrails
Blocking dependency for product positioning tasks (can't claim security without fixing leakage)

prior_decisions.decision: Separate product positioning (README) from live site validation (metadata audit)
rationale: README changes must complete before re-testing live site metadata
Live site may cache outdated metadata — requires deployment validation
Content accuracy depends on codebase-documentation parity
Wave 2 (content fixes) must complete before wave 3 (live validation)

prior_decisions.decision: Block final summary until all P0/P1 tasks pass review
rationale: Security findings report requires remediation evidence
Claims validation depends on code+docs parity
'Safe to push' verdict cannot be issued with open P0 security items
Product positioning changes affect legal/platform-policy risk

prior_decisions.decision: Use grep + manual verification for credential leakage audit (no secrets in repo history check)
rationale: Git history may contain historical credentials if LOG_LEVEL=INFO was used
grep cannot detect encrypted credentials in commit history
Manual git log inspection required for P0 security gate
Constraint: 'Verify no secrets in repo history' from objective

prior_decisions.decision: Assign weave-reviewer for all security tasks, weave-implementer for fixes
rationale: Security auditing requires zero-hallucination filter (weave-reviewer)
Code remediation requires TDD implementation (weave-implementer)
Credential redaction is code change, not configuration change
OWASP scanning + PRD compliance checks handled by weave-reviewer
